mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon, reflecting overall economic health.
failure_condition: If the 3-month annualized growth rate of RSAFS is positive, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a specific threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance, increasing the cost of capital and reducing corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investors discount future earnings more heavily.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS consistently remains above 4.5%, but the median 60-day forward return for SPX is positive in more than 40% of such occurrences over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of a significant rise in the unemployment rate and consecutive declines in non-farm payrolls signals a rapidly weakening labor market, which reduces consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as recession fears intensify.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS declines for two consecutive months, but SPX 20-day returns are positive in over 50% of these instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, accelerating industrial production indicates strong underlying economic activity and corporate demand, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as market sentiment improves due to robust output.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth exceeds 2% annualized over 3 months during an expansion, but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts below a critical level indicates a severe slowdown in the construction sector and broader economic demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon due to reduced investment and consumer confidence.
failure_condition: If HOUST falls below 1200K units, but the median 20-day forward return for SPX is positive in more than 45% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as real earnings decline.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 12-month change is above 3%, but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, sudden increase in the unemployment rate signals an unexpected deterioration in the labor market, creating immediate investor panic and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as economic uncertainty rises.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points in a single month, but SPX 5-day returns are positive in over 40% of these occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and robust retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength and consumer confidence, which supports higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as investors anticipate continued expansion.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth > 3% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4%
failure_condition: If both GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates meet their respective thresholds, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods over a 7-year rolling window.
